# Knowledge Gaps Analysis

> **Repo:** <!-- repo name -->  
> **Date:** <!-- date -->  
> **Assessed by:** <!-- human / AI / both -->

---

## Undocumented Modules

<!-- 2–4 bullets. Source areas with no comments, docs, or tests that reveal intent. Cite the module. -->

## Implicit Conventions

<!-- 2–4 bullets. Patterns applied consistently but never explained — naming rules, error handling style, data flow assumptions a contributor must infer. -->

## Single Points of Knowledge

<!-- 2–4 bullets. Complex or critical code with no redundant explanation — high bus-factor risk. -->

## Onboarding Blockers

<!-- 2–4 bullets. Things a new contributor must know that aren't discoverable from the repo: tribal knowledge, undocumented setup steps, required external context. -->

## Fragile or Opaque Areas

<!-- 2–4 bullets. Code that is difficult to change safely because invariants or expected behavior are unclear. -->

---

## Notable Observations

<!-- Patterns that compound the gaps (e.g. no tests AND no docs in the same module), areas that are surprisingly well-explained, or anything that would slow a new contributor most. -->

## Follow-up & Open Questions

<!-- Gaps that could only be confirmed by running the system or interviewing the original author, areas where a documentation sprint would have the highest ROI. -->
